pipeline_status.curmudgeon: 24 pending reviews digested. 18 assessed as stale (prior runs already patched targets). 3 new actionable items: SEC-4-intro verdict enumeration, WIN-010 formula typo, and ongoing WIN-010 moderate issues. 1 EXP created (EXP-163, SEC-6.5-FAIL004). internal_issues.description: SEC-1.9: defender escape that apparent-path suffices for single-observatory eclipse effects was unaddressed. EXP-162 P2 concedes WIN-010 case and anchors critique onto WIN-025/044/068.
SEC-1.9: n(r) is radial (horizontal disc distance), not altitudinal. EXP-162 P3 converts this into a strengthening argument.
SEC-4-intro: verdict enumeration omitted Misleading (22/70 WINs, the largest category). Patched this run.
WIN-010 detail_verdict_text typo: H(r) = 8537·exp(−1r/8619) → H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619). Stray leading 1 in exponent.
WIN-010: 3.2× FSF spread characterization conflates European range (includes Canary Islands) with within-20° claim. Needs reframe.
WIN-010: Meza et al. (2021, JGR Space Physics) citation unverified. Meza eclipse papers typically in JASTP not JGR.
WIN-010: Kp=3-4 (geomagnetically disturbed) overstates — G-scale disturbance starts at Kp 5. Need exact Kp value from NOAA/GFZ archive.
SEC-6.5-FAIL004: defense_survives=3. AAO proxy symmetry vulnerability + retroactive-refined citation. EXP-163 assigned to analyst.
Evaluation Guide (generate-html.js): '(045-051)' tidal range spans 7 IDs not 5. WIN-047/048 (non-tidal) are inside the range. Needs human edit to generate-html.js.

recommended_actions.action: Human: Fix generate-html.js line ~1402 — change '(045-051)' to explicit tidal WIN IDs '(045, 046, 049, 050, 051)' (ISS-994). WIN-047 Low-z Hubble and WIN-048 CMB are inside the range but are not tidal.
Analyst: ISS-990/991/992 (WIN-010 FSF spread, Meza citation, Kp value) — batch verify and patch. ISS-991 requires web search for correct Meza et al. citation details.
Analyst: EXP-163 (SEC-6.5-FAIL004 defense_survives=3) — add named magnetic WINs with short test windows to counter asymmetry claim; cite dome primary source for 'refined' label.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Eclipse critique hardened: dome's August 2026 test now faces a pre-test transparency challenge
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: Section 1.9 now explicitly challenges the dome to disclose before August 12, 2026 whether the Firmament Scaling Function amplitudes were computed with straight-line or bent-ray shadow geometry. Either answer damages the model — straight-line contradicts the refraction fix, bent-ray requires a derivation nowhere in the dome codebase. The section also now concedes that single-observatory eclipse measurements (WIN-010 BOU) don't need refraction-corrected geometry, strengthening credibility.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: Issues a concrete pre-registered transparency challenge with a deadline. Also demonstrates intellectual honesty by conceding the single-observatory case.
